title: Events / sec (1m)
description: Total OCSF events per second over the last 1 minute.
viz_content: SELECT count() / 60.0 AS events_per_sec FROM table(ocsf.ocsf_events) WHERE _tp_time > now() - interval 1 minute

title: Auth failures (5m)
description: Authentication failure count over the last 5 minutes.
viz_content: SELECT count() AS failures FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_authentication_flatten) WHERE status = 'Failure' AND _tp_time > now() - interval 5 minute

title: High+Critical findings (5m)
description: High and Critical severity security findings in the last 5 minutes.
viz_content: SELECT count() AS findings FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_security_finding_flatten) WHERE severity IN ('High','Critical') AND _tp_time > now() - interval 5 minute

title: Active source IPs (5m)
description: Distinct source IPs seen in authentication events over the last 5 minutes.
viz_content: SELECT count_distinct(src_endpoint_ip) AS unique_sources FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_authentication_flatten) WHERE _tp_time > now() - interval 5 minute

config.content:
### OCSF Security Event Analytics

Real-time analytics over the [Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework](https://schema.ocsf.io/) event stream emitted by the [`timeplus-ocsf-simulator`](https://pypi.org/project/timeplus-ocsf-simulator/) package.

Four event classes are flattened and surfaced above: **Authentication (3002)**, **Network Activity (4001)**, **Process Activity (1007)**, and **Security Finding (2001)**.

> Heads-up: the simulator generates random IPs and user names for each event, so cross-class correlations (e.g. linking an authentication failure to a subsequent suspicious process by IP) will not match. Each section above is self-contained per class.
